articles.title: About this blog
articles.description: "Idealism is the belief that everything can be perfect; pragmatism is the belief that we can make things better in the world we actually live in."
articles.contents:
Certainly far more than the so-called ‘Good Governance’ snake oil. Words shape our attitudes and our actions. Simple word changes can inform our guiding principles and improve our actions. With a few simple word changes districts would immediately benefit from higher quality, lower costs and less fruitless effort. The results would include much happier people; trustees, executive leadership, staff, teachers, parents, partners and students. Currently, the ‘wordsmiths’ now lump those flesh-and-blood human beings into the sterile corporate jargon of ‘stakeholders’. If you’re serious about making things better for your school district dig in. If you’re happy with ‘more of the same’ you can find plenty of that elsewhere.

“…management in any form is prediction.”
- Deming, W. Edwards. The New Economics for Industry, Government, Education (The MIT Press) (Function). Kindle Edition.


Warning: This is a callout for a warning.


Note: This is a general note.


Tip: Here’s a helpful tip.


Warning: Take caution with this advice.


Note: This is a custom callout styled with HTML.

It’s like…
trying to repair your car by reading the overall repair records of similar models. What you need is diagnostic data about what is happening (or not happening) with your specific vehicle.

articles.title: ***'When you want to help people you tell them the truth. When you want to help yourself, you tell them what they want to hear.' ~  Thomas Sowell***
articles.contents:
Uncommon Board Advice
          
          
          Home
          Blog
          Imagine If You Will...
          Opinion
          Resources
          About
          ☰
          
          
      
        
          
            
              
            
              ‘When you want to help people you tell
them the truth. When you want to help yourself, you tell them what they
want to hear.’ ~ Thomas Sowell
            
            
              
                
                    
                      
                         GitHub
                      
                    
                  
                                    
                    
                      
                         Email
                      
                    
                  
                                  
            
          
        
        
        
          
            
            
            
            Time Until TX Legislature Puts (at least) One More
            Nail in the Coffin of Public Schools (Sine die-last day of
            session)
            
            Why do legislatures everywhere inflict enormous,
            destructive pressures on school districts? As usual, the
            culprit is good intentions.
            Unfortunately, good intentions do not matter much when the
            practical effects destroy districts and their students.
            I intend to say and do something about that. Otherwise,
            public schools may be doomed.
            Every school board knows that many of these outside
            pressures are unnecessary. Some
            are destructive. All are
            imposed upon them. Far too many
            are unfunded.
            Even if you can you clearly identify those that are
            unnecessary, destructive, or unfunced for your district good
            luck with pursuading a legislator that their good intentions
            are toxic!
            Boards can also increase the burdens on their own
            districts. These are
            self-inflicted wounds. Adopting
            so-called ‘best practices’ frequently adds unnecessary
            burden on the district and volunteers.
            This blog is dedicated to minimizing
            “self-inflicted” wounds. The blog
            posts, essays and articles will (hopefully) provoke learning
            and debate. Please comment on them.
            Here you can talk about all of it, and what we can do
            about it
            (If you are a flamer, find another blog - you won’t last
            long here.)
            
            
            
            
            Why are we here?  To LEARN and to have FUN!
            
            Now, Let’s have some fun!
            
            
            
            SCUC-ISD Team of 8 (2024)
            
            
            
            Disclaimer:
            This blog is not an official publication of
            the Schertz-Cibolo-Universal City ISD. The contents are
            intended for informational and educational purposes only and
            does not represent the official position or policies of any
            school board or school district. The views and opinions
            expressed do not reflect the official stance of any
            educational institution or governing body. It is for those
            deeply concerned about the legislative ‘machinery’ that has
            become K-12 education in Texas, and the USA in general. Any
            data presented is either synthetic to illustrate a point, or
            is public information found on the Texas Education
            Association website. No effort has been made to ensure the
            accuracy of the information presented by those posting
            comments and the author assumes no responsibility for any
            errors or omissions, nor for any consequences arising from
            the use of this information. Readers are encouraged to
            verify any information they may rely on.
            
            
          
        
      
    

    
      
        
          
            
              
            
              ‘When you want to help people you tell
them the truth. When you want to help yourself, you tell them what they
want to hear.’ ~ Thomas Sowell

articles.title: Welcome to Dan's Rooftop
articles.description:
Welcome to our new blog, Dan's Rooftop. We hope you enjoy 
reading what we have to say!
